finding: Clock acquisition integrity must be represented separately from clock evaluation. Unsupported or malformed getOutputTimestamp data must not be normalized into valid zero-valued evidence.
design_implication: Capture raw browser timestamps and latency fields with explicit availability states, then invoke downstream evaluators only when acquisition is complete.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical implementation and tests before commit

uncertainty: No physical iPhone Safari execution was available in this run.
Specification conformance does not establish support in every browser version.
Clock consistency does not prove acoustic output or human audibility.
falsification_route: Run the sampler in the exact deployed browser session.
Compare explicit acquisition states across Safari and Chromium.
Reject the evidence packet when timestamp acquisition is incomplete or downstream classification is not consistent.
Pair accepted clock evidence with device-level or human audibility evidence before claiming audible output.
